old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-ozob","title":"Unbounded _windows dict in ReplayProtector enables memory exhaustion","description":"File: python/src/lichen/link/replay.py:139, 151-152

The _windows dict in ReplayProtector grows without bound:

    self._windows: dict[bytes | str | int, ReplayWindow] = {}
    ...
    window = ReplayWindow(self._window_size)
    self._windows[sender] = window

Each new sender gets a ReplayWindow entry. An attacker sending frames with unique sender IDs could exhaust memory.

Combined with the MIC stub accepting all frames, this is exploitable.

Fix: Add an LRU eviction policy or cap the number of tracked senders. Old/inactive senders should be evicted.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:21:57.180641Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:21:57.180679Z"}

    self._windows[sender] = window

Each new sender gets a ReplayWindow entry. An attacker sending frames with unique sender IDs could exhaust memory.

Combined with the MIC stub accepting all frames, this is exploitable.

Fix: Add an LRU eviction policy or cap the number of tracked senders. new_value: Implemented LRU eviction using OrderedDict in ReplayProtector (max_peers=32 matching Rust impl). Tests, ruff, and mypy all pass. Commit 4fb682861
